propositions: Build a benchmark that seeds exactly one defect per class into real cleanly-closed plans so the mutation itself is the label, avoiding circular LLM adjudication.
Five defect classes are mutated mechanically: absent asserted capability, layer-cake split, consumed-artifact-without-ordering-edge, unnecessary mechanism, and untagged operational AC.

The cleanly-closed filter requires post_claim_edit_class none, reopen_count zero, force_close false, completion_verifier_fail_count zero, had_persisted_review true, plus live closed status and ≥2 children for container classes.
E2 delivers the mutant corpus, labels, pre-registered thresholds, harness, and current-gate baseline as a reference point, not a pass claim — miss classes are expected near-zero baseline.

The counting rule credits advisory findings as detection, counts only blocking findings as control false positives, and treats INDETERMINATE verdicts as not-detected and non-FP, guarded by a 0.20 INDETERMINATE cap that refuses to emit a baseline when any class is invalid.

The benchmark runs the current gate over each mutant/control by redirecting ticket reads to a standalone git clone mutant store while grounding code against the real repo, ensuring the shared tickets branch HEAD is never modified.
